frameworks.name: categorical bias in working memory
frameworks.one_line: stored representations are pulled by the categorical structure of the stimulus space

frameworks.name: multivariate EEG decoding
frameworks.one_line: channel-based decoding of held features from scalp EEG during the delay

frameworks.name: decision/response-stage bias account
frameworks.one_line: biases arise at report rather than in memory representations

core_question: Are categorical biases present in the neural representations maintained in working memory, or do they emerge only at decision/response stages?
key_assumptions: multivariate EEG decoding indexes the content of working memory representations
categorical structure of the stimulus space can shape stored representations
response-preparation strategies can be experimentally suppressed
manipulations: remembered feature: spatial location and orientation
follow-up design that prevents response-preparation strategy
key_findings: spatial working memory decoding was biased away from the nearest cardinal location, matching behavioral biases
categorical decoding biases persisted when response preparation was prevented
biases were observed in EEG decoding prior to the behavioral report
interpretations: working memory representations themselves are categorically biased, not just the report stage
models of working memory must accommodate categorical structure of the representational space
